message:
Enhance tropical geometry and ultrametric attention demonstrations

Tropical Geometry (tropical_geometry_and_idempotent_algebra.py):
- Improve max-plus algebra implementation clarity
- Strengthen validation of tropical matrix operations
- Add detailed comments for piecewise-linear network construction
- Enhance route-level certificate computation
- Improve sparse mixture grid generation
- Add better error handling for degenerate cases

Max-Plus Algebra Enhancements:
The tropical semiring (ℝ ∪ {-∞}, max, +) implementation now includes:
- Explicit handling of -∞ identity element
- Validation of associativity and distributivity
- Proper broadcasting for batched operations
- Numerical stability checks for extreme values

Route Certificate Improvements:
The route-level certificate computation now tracks:
- Per-sample minimum margin (best vs second-best route)
- Per-node runner-up gaps along active routes
- Radius of certifiable robustness (min-gap/2)
- Node-wise minimum gap analysis

These certificates provide formal guarantees about model behavior:
  "Any perturbation with ||δ|| ≤ min_gap/2 cannot change the route"

This enables certified robustness analysis for tropical neural networks.

Sparse Mixture Grid:
Enhanced parameter-efficient mixture evaluation:
- Grid search over (k, λ) hyperparameters
- Accuracy vs sparsity tradeoff analysis
- Per-class sparsity tracking
- JSON export of full grid results

The grid sweep reveals optimal configurations for specific datasets,
balancing model capacity (k mixture components) with regularization (λ).

Piecewise-Linear Structure:
Improved documentation of how tropical operations naturally create
piecewise-linear functions:
  tropical_matmul(A, B)[i,j] = max_k(A[i,k] + B[k,j])

This is piecewise-linear in both A and B, with linear pieces
corresponding to different winning indices k in the max operation.

The demo now visualizes these linear regions and their boundaries.

Ultrametric Worlds (ultrametric_worlds_and_p_adic_computation.py):
- Refactor LCP (Longest Common Prefix) tree implementation
- Improve packed array representation for memory efficiency
- Add timing benchmarks for insert/query operations
- Enhance multi-head ultrametric attention
- Strengthen p-adic valuation computation
- Add variance reduction analysis

LCP Tree Optimization:
The packed array representation achieves O(1) prefix lookup:
- Per-level buckets stored contiguously
- Prefix sums enable constant-time rank queries
- Bit-packing for space efficiency
- Cache-friendly memory layout

Performance improvements:
  Before: O(log N) dict lookup per query
  After: O(1) array index with prefix sum

This is critical for attention scaling to 10k+ token sequences.

Scaling Analysis:
New benchmarks measure complexity empirically:
- Insert time vs N (sequence length)
- Query time vs N
- Memory usage vs N
- Occupancy per level (load factor)

Results confirm sub-quadratic scaling:
  Time: O(N log N) observed (vs O(N²) standard attention)
  Space: O(N) observed (vs O(N²) attention matrix)

Multi-Head Ultrametric Fusion:
Enhanced multi-head implementation with:
- Per-head ultrametric signatures
- Variance tracking across heads
- Head-wise attention distributions
- Fusion strategies (mean, max, learned)

Head variance analysis reveals that different heads learn different
hierarchical structures, similar to how standard attention heads
specialize in different linguistic features.

P-adic Tuner:
Simple hyperparameter tuning for p (base):
- Test p ∈ {2, 3, 5, 7} for different data distributions
- Measure attention entropy and head variance
- Select p maximizing separation in ultrametric space

Binary (p=2) works well for balanced hierarchies, higher p for
skewed distributions.

Constant-Time Operations:
Added demonstrations of O(1) operations enabled by packed arrays:
- rank(level, prefix): count of nodes with given prefix
- test(level, prefix): existence check
- occupancy(level): load factor per level

These primitives enable efficient pruning and rebalancing of the
trie during training.

Variance Reduction:
Analysis of multi-head variance reduction:
- Baseline variance: single-head attention entropy
- Proposed variance: multi-head fusion entropy
- Reduction delta: information gained by ensembling

Higher variance reduction indicates better head specialization.

Mathematical Connections:
Both demos bridge abstract mathematics and practical ML:

Tropical geometry:
  Abstract: Idempotent semirings, valuations
  Practical: Certifiable robustness, efficient inference

Ultrametric spaces:
  Abstract: p-adic numbers, non-Archimedean analysis
  Practical: Sub-quadratic attention, hierarchical routing

The enhanced implementations make these connections explicit through
detailed comments linking mathematical properties to algorithmic
implementations.

JSON Export:
Both demos now support rich JSON export with:
- Certificate arrays (tropical robustness margins)
- Timing profiles (ultrametric scaling curves)
- Variance metrics (multi-head analysis)
- Grid sweeps (hyperparameter optimization)

This enables systematic comparison across different mathematical
approaches and integration with automated experiment tracking.
